Comments: Note: The AKT3 rearrangement is a deletion of exons 11 - 13. One of the breakpoints is within exon 11. Functional significance is undetermined.

Note: The ARID5B rearrangement is an intragenic deletion of exon 4.
Note: The ARID5B rearrangement is an intragenic duplication of exon 10. One of the breakpoints is within exon 10. Functional significance is undetermined.

Note: The BCOR rearrangement is an intragenic inversion of exons 2-4. One of the breakpoints is within exon 4. The functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The BMPR1A rearrangement is a translocation with a breakpoint in exon 9. Functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The BMPR1A rearrangement is a deletion of exons 1-13. One of the breakpoints is within exon 13. The functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The BRCA2 rearrangement is an inversion of exons 1 - 24. Functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The CBFB rearrangement is an intragenic deletion with a breakpoint in exon 6. The functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The KMT2C rearrangement results in the deletion of exons 38 - 59. One of the breakpoints is within exon 59. Its functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The CDK8 rearrangement is an intragenic deletion with breakpoints in exon 6 and 7. The functional significance is undetermined.

Note: The CDKN2A rearrangement is a deletion of exon 2. One of the breakpoints is within exon 2. This affects the CDKN2Ap16INK4A (NM_000077) isoform.

Note: The CHEK1 rearrangement results in the deletion of exons 1-6. One of the breakpoints is within exon 6. The functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The CIC rearrangement is an intragenic inversion of exons 7-10. One of the breakpoints is within exon 10.
Note: The CIC rearrangement is an inversion of exons 1-12. One of the breakpoints is within exon 12.
Note: The DIS3 rearrangement results in the deletion of exons 1-16. The functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The DNMT1 rearrangement is a translocation that may result in a truncation of exons 37-40. The functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The DOT1L rearrangement is an intragenic deletion of exon 27. The functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The ETV6 rearrangement is an intragenic duplication of exons 4 - 5. Functional significance is undetermined.

Note: The MLL2 rearrangement is a intragenic duplication. The breakpoints are within exons 11 and 18. The functional significance is undetermined.

Site2_Description: Promoter of PEX6(-):16Kb from tx start
Comments: Note: The NOTCH4  Rearrangement results in the deletion of exons 1-23 of NOTCH4. Its functional significance is undetermined.

Note: The NTRK3 - DOT1L rearrangement is a translocation that results in a fusion of NTRK3 exons 1 - 6 to DOT1L exon 28. The fusion does not include the kinase domain of NTRK3. Functional significance is undetermined and further testing to determine the presence or absence of a targetable oncogenic fusion involving NTRK3 is required. This sample has been nominated for further analysis using the Archer targeted RNAseq assay. Archer will be performed and reported under a separate accession number if additional material is available.

Site2_Description: Promoter of SLC12A3(+): 910bp from tx start
Comments: Note: The NUP93 rearrangement is a deletion of exons 13-22.

Note: The PAX8 rearrangement is an inversion of exons 1 - 10. Functional significance is undetermined.

Note: The POLE - ANKLE2 rearrangement is a duplication that results in a fusion of POLE exons 1 - 9 to ANKLE2 exons 7 - 13. The fusion is predicted to be in frame. Functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The PPP2R1A rearrangement is a deletion of exons 1 - 7. One of the breakpoints is within exon 7.
Note: The PTPRD rearrangement is a deletion that includes PTPRD exons 30-46.

Event_Info: Duplication of 4 exons : out of frame
Comments: Note: The PTPRT rearrangement is an intragenic duplication of exons 2 - 5. Functional significance is undetermined.

Note: The RET rearrangement is an inversion with a breakpoint in intron 3 and could involve the exons 4-20, which include the kinase domain. The functional significance is undetermined. This sample has been nominated for further analysis using the Archer targeted RNAseq assay to help characterize the structural variant involving the RET gene. Assay will be performed if additional material is available and results will be reported under a separate accession number.

Note: The LEMD3 - RNF43 rearrangement is a translocaion which may result in the fusion of LEMD3 exons 1-7 with RNF43 exon 10. Its functional significance is undetermined. Multiple rearrangements involving RNF43 were detected in this sample and a more complex rearrangement involving RNF43 is possible. Additional testing by an alternative method is recommended, if clinically indicated.

Note: The SMARCA4 rearrangement results in the deletion of exons 2-32. The functional significance is undetermined.
Note: The STAT3 rearrangement is an intragenic inversion of exons 6 - 11. Functional significance is undetermined.
